info.name: VerifyStack — Full API (single collection)
info.description:
**One Postman file for everything** — public **API Gateway** only (`{{gatewayUrl}}`). Do **not** call `*.internal.*.azurecontainerapps.io` from Postman.

---
### Authentication (read this first)
| Folders | VerifyStack admin API key? |
|---------|----------------------------|
| **00**, **02**, **03**, **04**, **05**, **07**, **08** | **No.** No `X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key` on these requests (today’s repo behavior). Normal BGV + billing subscribe + notification diagnostics work without it. |
| **01**, **06** | **Yes.** Header **`X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key`** = `{{adminApiKey}}` (same as env **`NOTIFICATION_ADMIN_API_KEY`** / GitHub secret). Only **`/api/v1/billing/admin/**`** and **`/api/v1/notifications/admin/**`** need this. |

**Local docker:** if `NOTIFICATION_ADMIN_API_KEY` is unset, services default to **`dev-notification-admin-key`** — Postman’s `adminApiKey` matches that.

**Future:** You can put admin UIs behind your own login or gateway; **product integrations** can keep using **00 → 02 → 03 → 04 → 05** without ever setting `adminApiKey`.

---
### Run order
**00** Health → **01** Admin plan setup (operators) → **02** Subscribe → **03** BGV + consent → **04** Reports → **05** Notification smoke tests → **06** Admin templates & analytics (operators) → **07** Billing internals (debug) → **08** Local docker actuators only.

**KYC Hub mock (optional, not gateway):** `postman/KYC-Hub-Mock.postman_collection.json` — use when `KYC_HUB_MOCK=true` / Postman mock URL.

**Variables:** `planId`, `subscriptionId`, `reportId` (auto-set by scripts); `planFeatureId` (paste from **GET** `.../admin/plans` → `plan.features[].featureId` for folder **01** PATCH/DELETE feature). **`adminApiKey`** — only for folders **01** and **06**. `aggregationUrl`, `workerUrl`, … only for **08**.

---
### For UI / frontend — submit a BGV report (live API)

**1) Prerequisites (once per user)**
- Admin has created an **active** plan and attached every **feature** the UI can request (use `GET /api/v1/billing/admin/features` or catalog `check-feature-codes`).
- User has **subscribed**: `POST /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{planId}` with `userId`, `tenantId` (for individuals, **same email** for both is typical, e.g. `divyanshbhatt0@gmail.com`).
- `tenantId` on the subscription **must** match `tenantId` in the report body below (billing keys entitlement by tenant).

**2) Submit report**
- **Method/URL:** `POST {{gatewayUrl}}/api/v1/orchestrator/reports`
- **Headers:** `Content-Type: application/json` (no admin API key on this route).
- **Body (JSON):**
  - `requesterId` (string, required): signed-in account id — usually email.
  - `tenantId` (string, optional but **required for correct billing**): same as subscription `tenantId`; if omitted, backend uses `requesterId`.
  - `subjectFullName` (string, required)
  - `subjectPhone` (string, required)
  - `requestedChecks` (array of strings, required): **CheckType** enum names, uppercase with underscores.

**3) “Full verification” package =5 checks**
Use exactly:
```
["IDENTITY_PAN","IDENTITY_AADHAAR","EMPLOYMENT_EPFO","COURT_RECORD","CREDIT_REPORT"]
```
`CREDIT_REPORT` is billed as feature **`CIBIL_CHECK`** on the plan. All five features must exist on the user’s plan or the API returns **402 Payment Required**.

**4) Example (copy-paste body)**
```json
{
  "requesterId": "divyanshbhatt0@gmail.com",
  "tenantId": "divyanshbhatt0@gmail.com",
  "subjectFullName": "Ramesh Kumar Singh",
  "subjectPhone": "9876543210",
  "requestedChecks": [
    "IDENTITY_PAN",
    "IDENTITY_AADHAAR",
    "EMPLOYMENT_EPFO",
    "COURT_RECORD",
    "CREDIT_REPORT"
  ]
}
```

**5) Response:** `202 Accepted` with `{ "reportId": "..." }`. Then: **Grant consent** (`POST .../consent/{reportId}/grant`), poll pipeline (`GET .../aggregation/reports/{reportId}/pipeline`), load report (`GET .../reports/{reportId}`) — see folders **03** and **04**.

**Admin API key:** Only folders **01** and **06** — paths under **`/api/v1/billing/admin/**`** and **`/api/v1/notifications/admin/**`**. Orchestrator submit and user flows do not use this header.

variable.description: Kafka worker — no public API; local actuator only
Set by: Submit BGV Report → auto-populated
Signed-in user id (email for B2C) — must match subscription userId / tenant story
Billing tenant — must match CreateSubscription body tenantId (same as email for individuals)
Set by: 04b Get Report Detail → auto-populated
**Folders 01 + 06 only.** Same as NOTIFICATION_ADMIN_API_KEY. Not used for BGV flows (02–05). Header: X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key
Same as NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_API_KEY / VERIFYSTACK_NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_KEY — header X-VerifyStack-Internal-Key for folders 05f–05g (direct notification-webhook only)
SPA origin — must match bgv-auth FRONTEND_URL for real reset links; used in 05g sample body

item.name: 00 — Health Checks
item.description:
**No admin API key.** On **Azure**, only the **API Gateway** is publicly reachable — use these checks first.

Folder **08** uses `localhost`/`docker-compose` ports for deep debugging (not available from Postman against the cloud env).

item.name: 01 — Admin: Plan setup (API key required)
item.description:
**Operators only** — end-user apps skip this folder after the environment is configured.

Create a billing plan via **`/api/v1/billing/admin/**`**. Run once per environment. `planId` is auto-set by **1a**.

**Auth:** **`X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key: {{adminApiKey}}`**.

**Per-check metering:** one plan feature per `featureCode` from folder **06** (GET admin/features) or **00** (catalog). E.g. `CIBIL_CHECK` for UI check `CREDIT_REPORT`. Legacy `BGV_REPORT`-only plans will not satisfy batch entitlement.

request.description: Sets plan to INACTIVE (hidden from new checkout). Existing subscriptions are unchanged.
Omit JSON fields you do not want to change. Set `planFeatureId` from folder **06** → GET admin/plans → `plan.features[].featureId`.
Removes the feature from the plan definition. Does not remove existing subscription buckets.

item.name: 02 — User: Browse Plans & Subscribe
item.description:
**No admin API key.** Requester (HR, landlord, agency) browses active plans and subscribes.
`subscriptionId` is auto-set by **2b**.

item.name: 03 — BGV Core Flow
item.description:
**No admin API key.** The main verification pipeline. Run Step 1 then Step 2 in sequence.

**UI:** `requestedChecks` must be **CheckType** names (see collection description). Full package = all five: PAN, Aadhaar, EPFO, Court, Credit. **tenantId** must match the subscription created in folder 02.

What happens automatically after granting consent:
  bgv.consent.received
    → worker-identity: calls KYC Hub PAN/EPFO/Court APIs, publishes bgv.data.fetched per check
    → aggregation-service: scores when all checks arrive, publishes bgv.scoring.complete
    → report-generator: renders PDF, uploads to blob/tmp, publishes bgv.report.ready
    → notification-webhook: sends email + HTTP POST webhook

item.name: Step 1 — Submit BGV Report (via API Gateway)

exec: console.log('Now run: Step 2 — Grant Consent with this reportId');

request.description:
Submits a new BGV request.
- **No** `X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key` on this route.
- Billing **batch** reserve: one feature per check (e.g. `CREDIT_REPORT` → `CIBIL_CHECK`)
- `tenantId` must match folder **02** subscription (same email for B2C is typical)
- Returns **402** if plan lacks any requested feature
- Report is INITIATED; Kafka publishes `bgv.request.initiated`

Catalog: `GET {{gatewayUrl}}/api/v1/billing/catalog/check-feature-codes`

item.name: Step 1 (alt) — Submit BGV Report TENANT CHECK

request.description: Tenant / domestic staff check — only PAN + court checks (no employment).

item.name: Step 1 (alt) — Submit BGV Report DOMESTIC WORKER

request.description: Maid / domestic worker check.

Subject taps 'Allow' on the consent screen (simulating DigiLocker callback).
- Consent audit log is written to MongoDB
- `bgv.consent.received` (status=GRANTED) is published
- Triggers worker-identity → aggregation → report-generator → notification pipeline automatically

Subject declines consent. worker-identity receives DENIED status and stops. No checks are run.
The billing reservation will be released by the orchestrator on denial (future enhancement).

item.name: 04 — Dashboard & Report Endpoints
item.description:
**No admin API key.** Endpoints used by the frontend dashboard (dashboard/report.html).

Typical UI flow after granting consent:
  1. Poll 04a every 3s while pipelineStatus != COMPLETE  → show progress bar
  2. When COMPLETE → call 04b to get full report + scores  → render dashboard
  3. Call 04c to download/preview the PDF
  4. Call 04d for the requester's 'My Reports' table

request.description:
Returns real-time progress of the Kafka pipeline for a report.

Poll this while pipelineStatus is GATHERING or SCORING.
Switch to 04b when pipelineStatus = COMPLETE.

Response:
  pipelineStatus    : GATHERING | SCORING | COMPLETE
  expectedChecks    : all check types requested
  checksReceived    : checks that have come back from KYC Hub
  checksRemaining   : checks still in flight
  progressPercent   : 0-100

item.name: 04b. Get Report Detail (final scores + PDF link)

request.description:
Returns full report detail once generation is complete.

Response includes:
  overallStatus     : GREEN | AMBER | RED
  scoringBreakdown  : legacy five (identity, aadhaar, court, employment, creditReport) plus optional additionalScores map (CheckType → { flag, matchScore, remarks })
  requestedChecks   : check types that were run
  downloadUrl       : relative URL to fetch the PDF
  reportUrl         : direct blob URL (Azure SAS) or file:// path (local)
  generatedAt       : ISO timestamp

404 if not generated yet — check pipeline status first.

Downloads or redirects to the generated PDF.

Production (Azure Blob):   302 redirect to SAS URL → browser opens/saves directly
Local Docker fallback:     streams PDF bytes as application/pdf inline

202 if still GENERATING (check pipeline status first)
404 if reportId not found

query.description: The HR/landlord/agency who submitted the reports
Sort by generatedAt: asc | desc (default desc)
Optional ISO-8601 instant filter: generatedAt >= from (e.g. 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z)
Optional ISO-8601 instant filter: generatedAt <= to

request.description:
Paginated list of all reports for a requester — default newest first (sortDir=desc).
Optional filters: from / to bound generatedAt (inclusive). sortDir=asc for oldest first.

Response: Spring Page<ReportSummaryResponse>
  content[].reportId        : unique report ID
  content[].subjectFullName : subject name
  content[].overallStatus   : GREEN | AMBER | RED
  content[].status          : GENERATING | UPLOADED | FAILED
  content[].downloadUrl     : PDF download link
  content[].generatedAt     : ISO timestamp
  totalElements             : total count for pagination

item.name: 05 — Notifications & Diagnostics
item.description:
**No admin API key** on gateway routes here (repo default).

**Auth emails (welcome + forgot password):** bgv-auth calls notification-webhook with **`X-VerifyStack-Internal-Key`**. Set **`VERIFYSTACK_NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_KEY`** on auth = **`NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_API_KEY`** on notification-webhook. Set **`RESEND_API_KEY`** or SMTP on notification-webhook. **`NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN`** does not block auth emails.

**Test order:** (1) **05f** or **05g** direct to `{{notifUrl}}` to verify mail delivery; (2) **05h** forgot-password via gateway (LOCAL users only); (3) register new LOCAL user to trigger welcome.

Smoke-test notification-webhook without waiting for a full pipeline run.

Endpoints:
  POST /notifications/test-email   — send a test BGV-ready email (bypasses dry-run flag)
  POST /notifications/test-webhook — fire a test POST to any URL (e.g. webhook.site)
  GET  /notifications/logs/{id}    — see what was sent for a specific report
  GET  /notifications/logs?requesterId=&limit= — last N events; optional requesterId scopes to that user (email)
  GET  /reports/{id}/status        — lightweight PDF-ready check (no scoring payload)

**Pipeline (Kafka `bgv.report.ready`):** delivery respects `NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN` on notification-webhook. Infra / CI default is **`false`** so emails send when `RESEND_API_KEY` or SMTP is configured. If you see **`SKIPPED_DRY_RUN`** in logs, dry-run is still `true` in that environment.

Configure RESEND_API_KEY for HTTPS (recommended) or SMTP (Brevo/Gmail). Response includes delivery (resend vs smtp).

request.description:
Sends a real BGV-ready email to the specified address.

Bypasses the dry-run flag — even if NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN=true, this endpoint will attempt actual delivery.

Uses Resend when RESEND_API_KEY is set; otherwise SMTP. Verify sender domain in Resend for NOTIFICATION_FROM.

POSTs a bgv.report.ready event to any HTTPS URL.

Get a free inspection URL at https://webhook.site — paste the unique URL into webhookUrl.

Expected POST body delivered to your webhook:
{
  "event":          "bgv.report.ready",
  "reportId":       "...",
  "requesterId":    "test-requester",
  "subjectFullName":"Rahul Sharma",
  "overallStatus":  "GREEN",
  "reportUrl":      "http://...",
  "generatedAt":    "2026-04-04T..."
}

Returns all email + webhook log entries for a specific report.

Each entry shows:
  channel   : EMAIL | WEBHOOK
  status    : SENT | FAILED | SKIPPED_DRY_RUN
  recipient : email address or webhook URL
  sentAt    : ISO timestamp
  errorMessage : populated on failure

`SKIPPED_DRY_RUN` = `NOTIFICATION_DRY_RUN=true` (no real send). `SENT`/`FAILED` = dry-run off and provider attempted delivery.

query.description: Submitter email (or other requester id). Omit for global recent logs (ops).

request.description:
Returns the last N notification attempts, newest first.

Pass **requesterId** (same value as package submit `requesterId`, often an email) to list only that user's delivery events in one call. Omit **requesterId** to see recent events across all reports (operator health-check). **limit** defaults to 20, max 100.

Lightweight endpoint — returns just the generation status and whether the PDF is available.
Much cheaper to poll than the full GET /reports/{id}.

Response:
  status      : GENERATING | UPLOADED | FAILED
  pdfReady    : true when status=UPLOADED and reportUrl is set
  reportUrl   : local file path or Azure SAS URL
  downloadUrl : relative /reports/{id}/download path (null until pdfReady)
  generatedAt : ISO timestamp

Poll this every 5s after pipelineStatus=COMPLETE until pdfReady=true, then fetch the full detail.

**Not proxied by API gateway** — call **notification-webhook** on `{{notifUrl}}` (docker: `http://localhost:8087`).

Expect **204** if mail sends. On failure, read notification-webhook logs (`Auth email sent` / `Auth email failed`).

Requires: **`NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_API_KEY`** on webhook = **`notificationInternalKey`**; **Resend or SMTP** configured.
Same as **05f** — isolates Resend/SMTP + templates. Replace `demo-token...` with a real token from Mongo `password_reset_tokens` after **05h** if testing the full flow.

End-to-end path: auth saves a token and POSTs to notification-webhook.

**LOCAL accounts only** — Google sign-in users get no mail (by design). Unknown emails return the same success message (anti-enumeration).

If no email arrives: set **`VERIFYSTACK_NOTIFICATION_URI`** + **`VERIFYSTACK_NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_KEY`** on **bgv-auth**; match webhook **`NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_API_KEY`**; configure **RESEND_API_KEY** or **MAIL_*** on notification-webhook; align **`FRONTEND_URL`** on auth with your SPA (`frontendUrl`).

item.name: 06 — Admin: Auth templates, catalog & analytics (API key required)
item.description:
**Same auth as folder 01:** `X-VerifyStack-Admin-Key: {{adminApiKey}}`.

Use for operator dashboards: welcome / password-reset email HTML, billing feature catalog, plan list with subscriber counts, usage analytics. **Not** required for BGV product flows (02–05).

request.description:
Returns subjects, HTML bodies, optional `additionalTemplates` (id/subject/htmlBody), `updatedAt`, and `placeholdersHint`.

Placeholders — welcome: `{{recipientName}}`, `{{dashboardUrl}}`, `{{productName}}`. Password reset: `{{recipientName}}`, `{{resetLink}}`, `{{productName}}`. Custom rows: same `{{...}}` style.

Max lengths: subjects 300 chars; HTML bodies 200_000 chars. `additionalTemplates`: optional array (max 50); each `id` slug `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*` (max 64). Omit `additionalTemplates` to leave custom rows unchanged; send `[]` to clear.
Each row: plan + `activeSubscriberCount`.
Valid `featureCode` values to attach on plans (folder 01).
Admin table: shows deactivated catalog rows too.
Toggle whether **new** addFeature to plans is allowed for this code. Change path segment to another `featureCode`. Existing plan lines / buckets are not auto-removed.
Optional ISO-8601 `from`/`to` (default last 30 days). Optional `tenantId`.
Cursor pagination — enable `cursor` query for next page.

item.name: 07 — Billing Internals (Reference)
item.description:
**No admin API key.** These endpoints are called automatically by the orchestrator — not part of the normal manual flow. For debugging only.

**Batch:** orchestrator uses `POST .../entitlements/check-batch` with `tenantId`, optional `requesterId` (fallback when sub is keyed by userId), and `lines` (featureCode + count per check).

request.description: Mirrors orchestrator batch reserve. `requesterId` optional but helps when ACTIVE sub is on userId and tenantId alone does not match.

Reserves N units from the user's active subscription quota.
Returns a reservationId used to commit or release later.
count = number of requestedChecks in the report.
Called when report is successfully delivered — permanently deducts from quota.
Called when report initiation fails — returns reserved units to the quota pool.

item.name: 08 — Local docker only (direct ports)
item.description: **No admin API key.** Requires **localhost** (`docker-compose`). **Not used on Azure** — internal apps have no public ingress.
